remember: Students will be able to explain the different stages of economic development experienced by India.
Students will be able to recall the key features of the pre-independence, post-independence, and post-reforms periods in India's economic development.
Students will be able to identify the major factors that contributed to India's economic growth and development.
understand: Students will be able to understand the concept of comparative development experiences and its significance in the context of India.
Students will be able to comprehend the role of colonialism, independence, and reforms in shaping India's economic development.
Students will be able to analyze the impact of globalisation and liberalisation on India's economy.
apply: Students will be able to apply the concepts of comparative development experiences to understand the challenges faced by India in achieving sustainable economic growth.
Students will be able to use the knowledge of India's economic development to evaluate the effectiveness of different policies and interventions.
Students will be able to suggest potential solutions to address the developmental challenges faced by India.
analyze: Students will be able to critically analyze the strengths and weaknesses of India's economic development strategies.
Students will be able to evaluate the impact of India's economic development on its social and environmental outcomes.
Students will be able to compare and contrast India's economic development experience with that of other countries.

ncert_objectives: Explain the different stages of economic development in India.
Describe the major factors that contributed to India's economic growth and development.
Discuss the role of colonialism, independence, and reforms in shaping India's economic development.
board_exam_objectives: Explain the concept of comparative development experiences and its significance in the context of India (Class XI Economics, Chapter 2).
Describe the major factors that contributed to India's economic growth and development during the pre-independence, post-independence, and post-reforms periods (Class XI Economics, Chapter 2).
